 {"id":"bd-193","title":"E2E: Security regression suite (capability & sandbox)","description":"# Goal
Build **end-to-end security tests** that prove capability enforcement and sandbox boundaries with detailed logs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Test cases:
  - path traversal + symlink escape
  - forbidden host/network policy
  - env key denial
  - oversized payload / output limits
  - denied `exec` capability blocks `child_process.spawn` and `process.kill`
  - wasm bridge limits: memory/table limits, denied imports, trap mapping (PiWasm)
- Each failure must emit a structured log entry with capability, scope, and deny reason.
- Negative tests ensure no partial side effects occurred.

# Why (User Value)
- Trustworthy, repeatable proof that PiJS is safer than Node/Bun.

# Tests
- E2E scripts that run with verbose logging, produce a summary report, and archive per-test artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

 {"id":"bd-19rf","title":"Map extension discovery sources (official + community)","description":"Enumerate all discovery channels + exact repeatable queries (GitHub, OpenClaw/ClawHub, npm, awesome lists, blogs) to find the global set of popular Pi extensions.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:01:09.910054528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:31:25.135131617Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:31:25.135043473Z","close_reason":"Documented discovery channels + repeatable queries in docs/EXTENSION_CANDIDATES.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","research","sources"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-19rf","depends_on_id":"bd-29ko","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-19rf","depends_on_id":"bd-d7gn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3304,"issue_id":"bd-19rf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Goal
Produce a concrete list of discovery channels and query patterns for online research.

Include at minimum
- Official: pi-mono repo, examples, documentation, release notes
- Community: forks, “pi extension” GitHub search, blog posts, GitHub Topics
- Distribution: npm packages that mention pi extensions or Pi Agent integration
- Cross-refs: mentions in issues/PRs, curated lists

Output
A checklist of sources + exact search queries to run (so future agents can repeat the research deterministically).
","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:15:29Z"},{"id":3305,"issue_id":"bd-19rf","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"DISCOVERY CHANNELS + REPEATABLE QUERIES (2026-02-05)

Purpose
- Provide a deterministic “source checklist” so future agents can repeat discovery and converge on the same candidate set.

A) Official Pi sources (baseline)
- pi-mono examples/extensions README + dirs (already vendored once; keep as must-pass baseline)
- buildwithpi.ai packages + docs (if package JSON is exposed, treat it as authoritative metadata)
- badlogic gists tagged/mentioned as extensions

B) OpenClaw / marketplace ecosystems (new major surface)
- Identify the canonical OpenClaw repo/org and any associated “marketplace / hub / directory” (ClawHub or equivalent).
- Prefer machine-readable indexes (JSON feeds, GraphQL endpoints, API responses) over scraping HTML.
- Export raw dumps (with timestamps) so the inventory can be regenerated.

C) GitHub repo discovery (keyword-based)
Run repo searches, record top N results + reasons, and archive query strings.
- Query ideas (adjust to reduce noise):
  - "pi agent" extension
  - "buildwithpi" extension
  - "pi-mono" extension
  - "pi" "registerTool" extension
  - topic-based: topic:pi-extension OR topic:pi-agent OR topic:buildwithpi (if topics exist)

D) GitHub code discovery (signature-based)
Goal: find repos that contain *actual extension entrypoints*.
- Search for common registration patterns:
  - "registerTool(" AND ("export default" OR "ExtensionAPI" OR "ctx.")
  - "registerCommand(" OR "/"-command patterns
  - "registerProvider(" (custom providers)
  - "onEvent" / lifecycle hook names (session/tool/cancel)
  - "ui." / rpc UI calls used by extensions
- Heuristic: prefer hits in TypeScript/JavaScript; then validate as “true Pi extension” by checking for Pi protocol usage.

E) npm discovery
- Search npm for packages mentioning Pi Agent / buildwithpi / pi-mono / extension API.
- Capture download counts + dependents (popularity evidence).

F) Cross-reference mining (mentions)
- Search README/docs/issues across discovered repos for:
  - "pi extension" / "pi-agent extension" / "buildwithpi" / "pi-mono" mentions
  - Links to gists or extension bundles
- This tends to find “hidden” but widely used extensions.

Required output
- A list of sources + the exact queries executed (copy/paste ready).
- For each query: the date/time, tool used (GitHub UI/API/gh), and how many candidates it yielded.
- A “noise notes” section: which queries were too broad and what filters improved them.

 {"id":"bd-1hkm","title":"Task: Implement Tool Hostcall Handler (pi.tool)","description":"# Task: Implement Tool Hostcall Handler

## Objective

Implement the handler for HostcallKind::Tool that routes pi.tool() calls from extensions to the Rust ToolRegistry.

## Background

When an extension calls pi.tool("read", {path: "foo.txt"}), the flow is:
1. JS: pi.tool() enqueues HostcallRequest with kind=Tool
2. Rust: Dispatcher drains request, extracts tool name + params
3. Rust: Looks up tool in ToolRegistry, calls execute()
4. Rust: Converts ToolOutput to HostcallOutcome
5. JS: Promise resolves with result

## Implementation

```rust
impl ExtensionDispatcher {
    /// Handle pi.tool(name, input) hostcalls.
    /// 
    /// This allows extensions to invoke any tool in the registry,
    /// including built-in tools and other extension-registered tools.
    async fn dispatch_tool(
        &self,
        name: &str,
        input: serde_json::Value,
    ) -> HostcallOutcome {
        // Find tool in registry
        let Some(tool) = self.tool_registry.get(name) else {
            return HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "TOOL_NOT_FOUND".to_string(),
                message: format!("Tool '{}' not found in registry", name),
            };
        };
        
        // Generate unique tool_call_id for tracking
        let tool_call_id = format!("ext-{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string()[..8].to_string());
        
        // Execute the tool
        match tool.execute(&tool_call_id, input, None).await {
            Ok(output) => {
                // Convert ToolOutput to JSON Value
                let result = serde_json::json!({
                    "content": output.content,
                    "details": output.details,
                });
                HostcallOutcome::Success(result)
            }
            Err(e) => HostcallOutcome::Error {
                code: "TOOL_EXECUTION_ERROR".to_string(),
                message: e.to_string(),
            },
        }
    }
}
```

## Error Handling

| Error Condition | Code | Behavior |
|-----------------|------|----------|
| Tool not found | TOOL_NOT_FOUND | Return error outcome |
| Tool execution fails | TOOL_EXECUTION_ERROR | Return error with message |
| Invalid params | INVALID_PARAMS | Return validation error |
| Timeout | TIMEOUT | Return timeout error |

## Integration with Update Callbacks

The TypeScript reference supports streaming tool updates via the onUpdate callback. For v1, we can omit this (return final result only). Future enhancement:

```rust
// Future: Support streaming updates
let (tx, mut rx) = mpsc::channel(16);
let update_callback = Some(Box::new(move |update| {
    let _ = tx.try_send(update);
}));
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: Tool found and executed successfully
2. Unit test: Tool not found returns error
3. Unit test: Tool execution error propagates
4. Integration test: Built-in tool (read) via extension
5. Integration test: Extension-registered tool via another extension

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-389q (ExtensionDispatcher struct)
- Blocks: End-to-end extension tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] dispatch_tool() method implemented
- [ ] Tool lookup in registry works
- [ ] Successful execution returns Success outcome
- [ ] Errors return Error outcome with code + message
- [ ] Tool call IDs are unique
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:52:40.871031814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:51:28.199266466Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:51:28.199200443Z","close_reason":"Implemented ExtensionDispatcher dispatch_and_complete() for HostcallKind::Tool using ToolRegistry; added unit tests covering success + unknown tool rejection; gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hkm","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hkm","depends_on_id":"bd-389q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1i5x","title":"Implement pi.sendMessage() and pi.sendUserMessage() for message injection","description":"Allow extensions to inject messages into the conversation.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
// Custom message (not for LLM, optional display)
pi.sendMessage({
  customType: string,
  content: string,
  display?: boolean,
  details?: unknown
}, {
  triggerTurn?: boolean,
  deliverAs?: 'steer' | 'followUp' | 'nextTurn'
});

// User message (triggers LLM turn)
pi.sendUserMessage(content, {
  deliverAs?: 'steer' | 'followUp'
});
```

## Delivery Modes
- **steer**: Interrupt after current tool, skip remaining tool calls
- **followUp**: Queue after agent finishes current turn
- **nextTurn**: Default, processed as next user input

## Implementation Requirements
1. Hostcall handlers for 'sendMessage' and 'sendUserMessage'
2. Integration with agent message queues (steering_queue, follow_up_queue)
3. Custom message type in session entries
4. Display logic in TUI for custom messages

## Data Flow
1. Extension calls pi.sendMessage({customType: 'bookmark', content: 'Saved\!'})
2. Hostcall creates CustomMessage entry
3. If display=true, shows in TUI
4. If triggerTurn=true, queues for processing

## Test Plan
- Unit test: sendMessage creates correct entry type
- Unit test: sendUserMessage triggers agent turn
- Integration test: steer mode interrupts current processing
- Integration test: followUp mode queues correctly

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (hostcall handlers)
- src/extensions_js.rs (bridge to Rust)
- src/agent.rs (message queue integration)
- src/interactive.rs (display custom messages)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:41.477300174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:06:55.777896986Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:06:55.777832025Z","close_reason":"pi.sendMessage() and pi.sendUserMessage() both fully implemented as hostcalls in src/extensions.rs (line ~5531 and ~5606).  {"id":"bd-m84r","title":"Implement pi.session metadata APIs (setSessionName, getSessionName, setLabel)","description":"Allow extensions to manage session metadata.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
pi.setSessionName(name: string): void
pi.getSessionName(): string | undefined
pi.setLabel(entryId: string, label: string | undefined): void
```

## Use Cases
- Auto-naming sessions based on content
- Bookmarking important conversation points
- Extension-managed session organization

## Implementation Requirements
1. Hostcall handlers for setSessionName, getSessionName, setLabel
2. SessionInfoEntry for name changes
3. LabelEntry for entry labels
4. Integration with session picker (show names)

## Session Entry Formats
```json
// SessionInfoEntry
{"type": "session_info", "id": "...", "name": "My Feature Work"}

// LabelEntry
{"type": "label", "id": "...", "target_id": "abc123", "label": "important"}
```

## Test Plan
- Unit test: setSessionName creates SessionInfoEntry
- Unit test: getSessionName returns current name
- Unit test: setLabel creates LabelEntry
- Integration test: session picker shows custom name

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (hostcall handlers)
- src/session.rs (entry types if needed)
- src/session_picker.rs (display names)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:06.120728084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:52:07.968156191Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:52:07.968094526Z","close_reason":"All three session metadata APIs implemented: (1) pi.getSessionName() - JS bridge __pi_get_session_name (extensions_js.rs:2980) calls pi.session('get_name') Rust handler (extensions.rs:5494).  {"id":"bd-m9rk","title":"No-mock: extensions message/session tests use real Session","description":"# Goal
Remove in-memory/session mocks from extension message/session tests and use real `Session` + `SessionHandle` paths.

# Background
`tests/extensions_message_session.rs` defines an in-memory session mock and `tests/e2e_message_session_control.rs` uses `load_extension_with_mocks`. This conflicts with the no-mock policy for core paths; we should exercise the real session plumbing and serialization behavior instead.

# Scope
- Replace in-memory session mock with real `Session` + `SessionHandle` backed by a temp directory.
- Use real extension fixtures (from `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/**`) instead of mock extension strings where feasible.
- Validate session mutation APIs: append message, set label/name, add custom entry, get branch entries.
- Emit JSONL logs + artifacts (session JSONL, fixture id, event sequence).

# Files
- `tests/extensions_message_session.rs`
- `tests/e2e_message_session_control.rs`
- `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/**` (fixtures)

# Acceptance
- Tests run against real session persistence (JSONL) and real extension artifacts.
- No in-memory mock session types remain in these tests, unless explicitly allowlisted with rationale in `docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:26:46.911824942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:34:14.146134401Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:34:14.146002815Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m9rk","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-nh33","title":"E2E logs: extension registration JSONL + artifacts","description":"# Goal

- Wire `TestHarness` logging to emit JSONL logs and artifact index for each test case.

- Include redaction summary and deterministic ordering.

- `tests/common/harness.rs` (only if helper additions are needed)

- JSONL logs + artifact index are produced for all tests in this file.

 {"id":"bd-nom.1","title":"Hostcall ABI: WASM host.call Uses Shared Dispatcher (Parity Refactor)","description":"# Goal
Refactor the WASM component hostcall path (`host.call(name, input_json)` in `src/extensions.rs` `mod wasm_host`) so it uses the shared hostcall dispatcher:

`HostCallPayload (from JSON) -> dispatch_host_call_shared() -> HostResultPayload -> WIT return string`

# Current State (Observed)
- WASM hostcalls are implemented in `src/extensions.rs` under `mod wasm_host`, in `impl host::Host for HostState { async fn call(...) }`.
- The current implementation:
  - parses JSON into `HostCallPayload`
  - validates capability mismatch
  - performs policy + logging + timeout
  - dispatches by method with bespoke logic
- A shared dispatcher already exists (`dispatch_host_call_shared()`), but WASM hostcalls do not yet call it.

# Scope
1. Keep input validation that is specific to the WIT contract:
   - `name` must match `payload.method` (or explicitly document any tolerated mismatch).
   - JSON parse errors are `invalid_request`.

2. Replace the bespoke WASM dispatch path with:
   - Build `HostCallContext`:
     - `runtime = "wasm"`
     - `extension_id` (from registration state)
     - `policy`, `tools`, `http`, `fs`, `env_allowlist`, `manager`
   - Call `dispatch_host_call_shared()`.

3. Convert `HostResultPayload` to the WIT return type:
   - On success: return `Ok(<serialized output JSON>)`.
   - On error: return `Err(<serialized HostCallError JSON>)`.
   - Ensure error codes are taxonomy-only (shared dispatcher guarantees this).

4. Ensure logging is not duplicated:
   - `dispatch_host_call_shared()` already emits `host_call.start/end` and `policy.decision`.
   - Remove duplicate logging from the WASM entrypoint so we don’t double-count or drift.

# Tests
- `--features wasm-host` unit/integration tests:
  - capability mismatch -> `invalid_request`
  - denied -> `denied`
  - timeout -> `timeout` with retryable
  - session/ui/events with manager absent -> deterministic taxonomy
  - params_hash parity vs JS for canonical calls (tie into `bd-1uy.1.4`)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No duplicated policy/timeout/logging logic remains in the WASM hostcall entrypoint.
- [ ] WASM hostcall errors and success outputs match the shared dispatcher’s semantics.
- [ ] `cargo test --all-targets --features wasm-host` is green.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightHeron","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:57:36.997165900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T11:21:28.916105960Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T11:21:28.916075122Z","close_reason":"Completed: WASM host.call uses shared dispatcher; wasm-host gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","hostcall","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nom.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-nom.1","depends_on_id":"bd-nom","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"}]}
